about: Per-zone plantar load norms + ANATOMICAL positions (mm) + kinetic-CHAIN injury links, per sport/movement. Used to flag OVER/UNDER-used foot regions vs the field and the injuries (foot-local AND proximal) they predict. Every value carries a confidence flag: 'published' (stated in a cited paper), 'published_order' (rank ordering from a paper; magnitudes filled to match), or 'estimated' (biomechanically reasonable, no direct source yet). Add sources -> the DB gets more rigid.

foot_reference.axes: x = medial(0) -> lateral(width); y = heel(0) -> toe(length)
foot_reference.note: positions below are for a 255 mm reference foot; scale by YOUR foot length (x scales with width ~= 0.37 x length). zone_load.py --foot-length-mm does this.

sensor_map.note: our 8-FSR rig senses these directly; met2 & met4 are INTERPOLATED from neighbors.

gym_grf.cite: Biomechanics of landing in gymnasts: a scoping review, Front. Sports Act. Living, 2025

niu2014.cite: Niu et al., BioMed Research International, 2014

run_press.cite: Foot orthoses & forefoot peak plantar pressure in running, 2016

run_region.finding: central met heads > 1st met > heel > 1st toe > lateral mets

skate_grf.cite: Landing GRF in figure skaters; ACSM 'Science of Figure Skating: Jumps'

dance_press.cite: Foot deformity & 1st MTP plantar pain in DanceSport, Children, 2022
dance_press.finding: 1st-MTP pain -> less hallux, more met-head load

met2_injury.finding: 2nd met = #1 stress-fracture site; base of 2nd met = 'dancer's fracture'

hsi_sprint.cite: Mechanism of hamstring strain in sprinting (PMC6188997); Soccer HSI video review, Front. Public Health, 2026
hsi_sprint.finding: late-swing eccentric strain JUST BEFORE foot-strike; overstride plausibly worsens it; #1 soccer injury

acl_grf.cite: ACL landing biomechanics predict re-injury, Am J Sports Med (PMC4920967); drop-vertical-jump GRF
acl_grf.finding: high vGRF + loading rate at landing predicts ACL; ACL strain peaks with vGRF

acl_asym.cite: Limb symmetry & landing mechanics after ACLR, PMC8359711
acl_asym.finding: L/R asymmetry >10-15% is a non-contact ACL precursor / return-to-sport flag

pose_grf.cite: Estimation of vGRF in single-leg landing from 2D video + pose-estimation AI, 2024
pose_grf.finding: 2D-pose -> vGRF is feasible; basis for the video_force connector

chain_rules._note: foot-loading SIGNATURES -> kinetic-chain injuries (foot-local AND proximal). It's all connected to the foot.

chain: ACL (vGRF peak co-times with ACL strain)

overstride_forefoot_late.signal: foot lands well ahead of the body in sprint

running_rearfoot.chain_injury: over-stride -> hamstring (late-swing); medial collapse -> shin splints / PF

running_forefoot.chain_injury: Achilles / calf (forefoot loading); central mets carry most

gymnastics_landing.injury: central-met & heel stress from very high BW impacts
gymnastics_landing.chain_injury: high vGRF -> ACL / tibial stress; stiff landings raise force

figure_skating_landing.movement: single-leg jump landing, back outside edge
figure_skating_landing.injury: lateral-column & central-met overload, navicular (60-100 landings/day, one leg)
figure_skating_landing.chain_injury: single-leg repetition -> hip/knee asymmetry; loading-rate on the landing leg

ballet_releve.injury: 1st-MTP overload -> hallux valgus / sesamoiditis; hallux UNDER-load shifts to met2 (dancer's fracture)
ballet_releve.chain_injury: forefoot bias -> Achilles / FHL tendon; posterior chain

ballet_pointe.injury: base of 2nd-met stress fx ('dancer's fracture'); hallux/1st-ray load extreme
ballet_pointe.chain_injury: COG shifts onto met shafts -> ankle / posterior-tibial

soccer_sprint.chain_injury: **HAMSTRING strain** — late-swing eccentric just before foot-strike (#1 soccer injury); over-stride (foot lands far ahead) lengthens the eccentric load

soccer_cut.chain_injury: **non-contact ACL** — deceleration + knee valgus + L/R asymmetry >10-15%; compare the plant leg to the other side

jump_landing.injury: central-met & bone stress from repeated impacts
jump_landing.chain_injury: **ACL** — high vGRF + loading rate + L/R asymmetry are the flags; land soft & symmetric
